bundle_catalog.title: Closure Guardrails
bundle_catalog.description: Closure facts are real on the public corpus, but the prompt should only allow exact, traceable closure references.

bundle_catalog.title: Fresh-Variable True Override
bundle_catalog.description: Fresh variables should bias search toward FALSE, but they do not block TRUE when there is an exact derivation.

bundle_catalog.title: Safe True Core
bundle_catalog.description: Most public TRUE cases are rewrite-backed and should be expressed as explicit short derivations.

bundle_catalog.title: False Witness Routing
bundle_catalog.description: Projection and fresh-variable cues dominate FALSE search and should terminate early when LP/RP separates.

bundle_catalog.title: Finite Witness Core
bundle_catalog.description: Compact explicit finite witnesses cover most remaining public FALSE cases once projection is checked.

bundle_catalog.title: Singleton Shortcut
bundle_catalog.description: Singleton-strength E1 equations give a high-yield, low-risk TRUE shortcut when applied literally.

bundle_catalog.title: Invariant False Lane
bundle_catalog.description: Canonizer and invariant-based refutations are real, but only safe when the invariant is stated concretely.

bundle_catalog.title: Finite Witness Escalation
bundle_catalog.description: A small tail of FALSE cases needs structured finite tables after the compact witness ladder fails.

selected_bundles.title: False Witness Routing
selected_bundles.description: Projection and fresh-variable cues dominate FALSE search and should terminate early when LP/RP separates.

selected_bundles.title: Finite Witness Core
selected_bundles.description: Compact explicit finite witnesses cover most remaining public FALSE cases once projection is checked.

selected_bundles.title: Invariant False Lane
selected_bundles.description: Canonizer and invariant-based refutations are real, but only safe when the invariant is stated concretely.

selected_bundles.title: Safe True Core
selected_bundles.description: Most public TRUE cases are rewrite-backed and should be expressed as explicit short derivations.

selected_bundles.title: Singleton Shortcut
selected_bundles.description: Singleton-strength E1 equations give a high-yield, low-risk TRUE shortcut when applied literally.

selected_bundles.title: Closure Guardrails
selected_bundles.description: Closure facts are real on the public corpus, but the prompt should only allow exact, traceable closure references.

selected_rules.text: [S01 fresh_var] If E2 introduces fresh variables not in E1, treat that as a FALSE-search trigger: test LP/RP first and do not drift into a TRUE guess from shape alone.
[F10 projection_family] If E1 preserves the left or right boundary variable and E2 breaks it, test LP/RP immediately; if one separates, answer FALSE with that named witness.
[S02 projection_first] When left/right boundary variables differ, test LP then RP before any broader finite witness search.
[S09 projection_witness] If LP or RP satisfies E1 but falsifies E2, stop there and output FALSE with that named witness and assignment.
[S04 anti_fake_false] FALSE requires one explicit witness, with a named operation or table and an assignment/check showing E1 holds and E2 fails.
[F11 small_finite_magma] After LP/RP, try compact witnesses in a fixed ladder (C0, XOR, XNOR, AND, OR); FALSE needs one named magma and assignment showing E1 true, E2 false.
[S10 finite_ladder] If projection fails, try C0, XOR, XNOR, AND, OR in that order and keep the first explicit separating witness.
[S06 finite_scope] Never promote a finite-only counterexample family into a general implication claim.
[F30 canonizer_confluence] Use this FALSE lane only when you can name the invariant or normal-form feature that E1 preserves and E2 breaks.
[S12 canonizer_guard] Use canonizer/confluence only with a named invariant or normal-form mismatch that E1 preserves and E2 breaks.
[F20 rewrite_metatheorem] Answer TRUE only from an explicit substitution or short rewrite chain from E1 to E2; variable-count similarity or failed witness search is not enough.
[S05 anti_fake_true] TRUE requires an exact rewrite chain, singleton-strength collapse, or exact closure chain; 'no witness found' is never enough.
[S08 rewrite_chain] For TRUE, write a short concrete substitution/rewrite chain, not broad algebraic commentary.
[F01 singleton_rewrite] If E1 is literally x = T and x is absent from T, use that singleton-strength collapse explicitly and answer TRUE.
[S07 closure_exact] Closure language is only safe when you can name the exact backbone step or chain carrying E1 to E2; otherwise fall back to a direct rewrite.

[S02 projection_first] When left/right boundary variables differ, test LP then RP before any broader finite witness search.
[S09 projection_witness] If LP or RP satisfies E1 but falsifies E2, stop there and output FALSE with that named witness and assignment.
[S04 anti_fake_false] FALSE requires one explicit witness, with a named operation or table and an assignment/check showing E1 holds and E2 fails.
[F11 small_finite_magma] After LP/RP, try compact witnesses in a fixed ladder (C0, XOR, XNOR, AND, OR); FALSE needs one named magma and assignment showing E1 true, E2 false.
[S10 finite_ladder] If projection fails, try C0, XOR, XNOR, AND, OR in that order and keep the first explicit separating witness.
[S06 finite_scope] Never promote a finite-only counterexample family into a general implication claim.
[F30 canonizer_confluence] Use this FALSE lane only when you can name the invariant or normal-form feature that E1 preserves and E2 breaks.
[S12 canonizer_guard] Use canonizer/confluence only with a named invariant or normal-form mismatch that E1 preserves and E2 breaks.
[F20 rewrite_metatheorem] Answer TRUE only from an explicit substitution or short rewrite chain from E1 to E2; variable-count similarity or failed witness search is not enough.

[S07 closure_exact] Closure language is only safe when you can name the exact backbone step or chain carrying E1 to E2; otherwise fall back to a direct rewrite.
[S03 structured_finite] Only after LP/RP and canned witnesses fail should you escalate to 4x4 or structured finite tables; still report one explicit witness.
[F13 all4x4_tables] Use 4x4 tables only as an escalation after easier witnesses fail, and still report one explicit separating table/assignment.

selected_rules.text: [S01 fresh_var] If E2 introduces fresh variables not in E1, treat that as a FALSE-search trigger: test LP/RP first and do not drift into a TRUE guess from shape alone.
[F10 projection_family] If E1 preserves the left or right boundary variable and E2 breaks it, test LP/RP immediately; if one separates, answer FALSE with that named witness.
[S02 projection_first] When left/right boundary variables differ, test LP then RP before any broader finite witness search.
[S09 projection_witness] If LP or RP satisfies E1 but falsifies E2, stop there and output FALSE with that named witness and assignment.
[S04 anti_fake_false] FALSE requires one explicit witness, with a named operation or table and an assignment/check showing E1 holds and E2 fails.
[F11 small_finite_magma] After LP/RP, try compact witnesses in a fixed ladder (C0, XOR, XNOR, AND, OR); FALSE needs one named magma and assignment showing E1 true, E2 false.
[S10 finite_ladder] If projection fails, try C0, XOR, XNOR, AND, OR in that order and keep the first explicit separating witness.
[S06 finite_scope] Never promote a finite-only counterexample family into a general implication claim.
[F30 canonizer_confluence] Use this FALSE lane only when you can name the invariant or normal-form feature that E1 preserves and E2 breaks.
[S12 canonizer_guard] Use canonizer/confluence only with a named invariant or normal-form mismatch that E1 preserves and E2 breaks.
[F20 rewrite_metatheorem] Answer TRUE only from an explicit substitution or short rewrite chain from E1 to E2; variable-count similarity or failed witness search is not enough.

[S07 closure_exact] Closure language is only safe when you can name the exact backbone step or chain carrying E1 to E2; otherwise fall back to a direct rewrite.
[S11 fresh_var_true_guard] Fresh variables in E2 do not block TRUE if you can explicitly derive E2 from E1; without that derivation, stay in FALSE-search mode.
